questions.question: When the heating degree day method was developed, fuel was _____ and buildings were _____ insulated.
It is relatively impossible to assess the location and magnitude of all the air leakage paths in a typical building.
The U-value of any material or assembly is simply the reciprocal of the ____ of that material or assembly.

Since the walls, floors, and ceilings of buildings are rarely constructed of a single material, it is often necessary to determine the ______ of an assembly made up of several materials in contact with each other.
The rate at which domestic hot water is used at the fixtures within a building varies considerably with the type of ____, and the time of day.
To estimate the annual space heating cost provided by an electrically operated heat pump, its average COP must be factored in. What does COP stand for?
The contour lines with temperatures labeled in °C are called _____.
An object's thermal ____ can be defined as its thickness in the direction of heat flow divided by its thermal conductivity.
Many residential and commercial buildings have concrete floor slabs rather than crawl spaces or full basements. Downward heat losses are relatively large from interior areas of the floor slab.
An important aspect of domestic water-heating loads is the peak hourly demand.
In addition to conduction losses, heat is also carried out of buildings by uncontrolled air leakage. This is called ____ heat loss.
The proper approach is to perform a heat load calculation ____.
All electric, gas, and oil-fired storage type water heaters sold in the United States have a _____.
The R-value of a material is _____ dependent on the material’s temperature.
The U-value of any material or assembly is simply the reciprocal of the R-value of that material or assembly.
One method of approximating the heat loss through basement walls is based on dividing the wall into ___ strips based on the height of finish grade.
The R-value of a material is directly proportional to its thickness.
The heating load is ____ of heat flow from the building to the outside air.
In addition to the thermal resistances of the solid materials, there are two thermal resistances, called the ____ film resistance and ____ film resistance.
One can assume that individual room heating loads are proportional to room floor area.
All building surfaces that separate heated space from unheated space (walls, windows, ceilings, doors, foundation, etc.) are called the ____ of the building.
To compensate for the effect of internal heat gains and warm-weather degree days, ASHRAE devised a correction multiplier known as _____.
One common method of factoring local weather conditions into estimates of heating energy use is based on heating ____.
The gallons of hot water supplied as the first-hour rating are based on a ___ °F temperature rise between the incoming cold water and the leaving hot water.
The number of heating degree days that accumulate in a 24‑hour period is the difference between 65°F and the average outdoor air temperature during that period.
